purpose: Repository-verification canon for Keon Cortex v2. Converts Cortex product/capability claims, including Evidentiary Memory, two-plane storage, Epoch Roots, Gravitational Recall, replayable/counterfactual recall, and Collective bridge artifacts, into evidence-backed implementation, test, CLI, CI, and proof-artifact requirements for verify-canon.

included: Cortex memory substrate
deterministic shard identity
append-only memory and causal lineage
deterministic replay and outbox durability
tenant isolation
authoritative document store and derivative vector index separation
decay and reinforcement semantics
governed signing and evidence pack verification
Cortex proof spine I1-I7
proof bundle and proof CLI commands
benchmark CLI and deterministic benchmark artifacts
CI gates for deterministic proof and benchmark artifacts
developer tooling hygiene needed to keep proof lanes reliable
Evidentiary Memory category claims and Verifiable Memory alias consistency
two-plane storage architecture with immutable spine records and mutable gravity index
IMemory recall lens with content-hash verification metadata
Epoch Root attestation tree with offline inclusion proofs
tamper-evident hash tree construction and test vectors
Gravitational Recall v1 fixed-force scoring model
hash-pinned gravity parameters and explicit asOf scoring inputs
recall events as spine records
historical recall replay and counterfactual recall purity
Collective deliberation records as cognitive spine artifacts
heat-as-trust-anchor policy input bridge without receipt verification dependency
doctrine vocabulary lint for transparency log, attestation, and tamper-evident hash tree terminology
excluded: Keon Systems Runtime policy evaluation or execution governance internals
Keon MCP Gateway transport/auth implementation internals
public website page rendering except Cortex claim consistency
commercial packaging or licensing enforcement unless it changes governed Cortex behavior

integration_boundaries.relationship: Cortex may preserve or export receipt-linked memory/evidence artifacts; Runtime owns policy decision/execution authority.
integration_boundaries.verification_rule: Do not require Cortex to perform Runtime governance decisions. Reject any Cortex claim or code path that presents Cortex memory, summaries, vector hits, or proof artifacts as authorization authority.

integration_boundaries.relationship: Gateway may emit receipts and structured governed envelopes that Cortex stores or references.
integration_boundaries.verification_rule: Cortex must preserve receipt lineage when provided, but gateway auth, MCP protocol handling, and Decide/Execute enforcement are out of Cortex scope.

integration_boundaries.relationship: Collective may use Cortex as canonical causal memory substrate and may store deliberation records as cognitive spine artifacts.
integration_boundaries.verification_rule: Collective planning, branches, heat, and deliberation remain cognitive and non-authoritative. Cortex may preserve them, expose heat as policy-evaluable input, and link deliberation refs, but receipt verification and Runtime authorization must never require or consult deliberation records.

source_product_canon.note: Product canon is claim input. This verification canon is the stricter repo-evidence contract.

verification_principles: Claims require receipts or inspectable evidence.
Documentation and README claims are not implementation proof.
Test names are not proof unless assertions verify the claimed invariant.
Proof artifacts must be deterministic, portable, and byte-identical where claimed.
Vector index state is derivative and must never outrank authoritative document records.
Tenant isolation failures must fail closed.
Memory lineage and replay claims require negative and restart/retry evidence.
Governed signing claims require tamper, revoked-key, retired-key, and manifest integrity tests.
Cortex must not claim Runtime execution governance ownership.
Ambiguous evidence is failing evidence in strict mode.
Evidentiary Memory claims require memory records that can testify through inspectable spine records, content hashes, epoch roots, and replay evidence.
Gravity may order recall but must never gate access to immutable spine records.
Every scoring, heat, and selection function must be versioned and hash-pinned.
Hidden randomness and hidden wall-clock time are forbidden in proof-critical recall scoring paths.
Epoch roots must preserve closed historical roots; late arrivals are attested forward, never backfilled by root mutation.
Cognitive records, deliberation narratives, witness material, and heat signals are never execution receipts and never satisfy receipt verification paths.
Use doctrine vocabulary: tamper-evident hash tree, transparency log, attestation. Do not use blockchain, ledger, or chain in code identifiers, comments, or docs.

canonical_invariants.name: Deterministic memory and replayable core proof summary
canonical_invariants.summary: Core memory state and proof summaries are deterministic and reconstructable from canonical substrate.

canonical_invariants.name: Fail-closed tenant isolation
canonical_invariants.summary: Tenant context is mandatory, non-aliased, and enforced across write/query/provider-result boundaries.

canonical_invariants.name: Replay and outbox durability
canonical_invariants.summary: Outbox leasing, retry, restart, replay, and idempotent processing preserve writes and deterministic state.

canonical_invariants.name: Authoritative store over derivative index
canonical_invariants.summary: Document store is canonical truth; vector index is disposable, replayable, and unable to corrupt authority.

canonical_invariants.name: Deterministic shard identity and idempotent ingestion
canonical_invariants.summary: Equivalent canonical payloads produce same shard identity, distinct payloads produce distinct identities, and duplicate ingestion no-ops.

canonical_invariants.name: Decay and reinforcement correctness
canonical_invariants.summary: Decay/reinforcement is deterministic, versioned, bounded by trust floors/ceilings, and cannot promote poisoned memory by mass alone.

canonical_invariants.name: Governed signing and Evidence Pack integrity
canonical_invariants.summary: Governed mode signs/verifies receipts, rejects tampering and revoked keys, accepts retired historical keys per semantics, and verifies deterministic Evidence Packs.

canonical_invariants.name: Evidentiary Memory two-plane separation
canonical_invariants.summary: Immutable spine records remain canonical while mutable gravity weights only order recall and never remove, authorize, or rewrite truth.

canonical_invariants.name: Epoch Root attestation and offline inclusion proof
canonical_invariants.summary: Event content hashes roll into deterministic spine, day, week, and quarter roots with offline-verifiable inclusion proofs and no recomputation of closed epochs.

canonical_invariants.name: Gravitational Recall deterministic scoring
canonical_invariants.summary: Occurrence, recall, recency, and association forces produce deterministic, versioned, hash-pinned recall scores from explicit inputs.

canonical_invariants.name: Replayable and counterfactual recall
canonical_invariants.summary: Historical recall decisions can be reconstructed exactly from spine records and evaluated under read-only counterfactual scenarios.

canonical_invariants.name: Collective bridge preserves cognition without authority
canonical_invariants.summary: Deliberation records and heat signals may be preserved and evaluated, but they never become receipts or execution authorization authority.

requirements.title: Deterministic shard identity
requirements.summary: Shard identity must be canonical SHA-256 based: equivalent canonical payloads map to the same identity and semantically distinct payloads map to distinct identities.
verification_evidence_required: Tests for field-order and JSON-order variation
minimum_tests_required: Equivalent payloads produce identical shard IDs
Distinct payloads produce distinct shard IDs
Double-run proof artifact is byte-identical

requirements.title: Idempotent ingestion no-duplicates
requirements.summary: Duplicate/retried ingestion must no-op without creating duplicate authoritative shards or duplicate output records.
verification_evidence_required: Unique identity constraint or equivalent idempotency guard
minimum_tests_required: Duplicate ingest creates one authoritative shard
Retry/replay preserves single output record
Concurrent/repeated ingestion does not produce duplicates where supported

requirements.title: Append-only authoritative memory model
requirements.summary: Authoritative memory state must preserve historical lineage through append/replay behavior and must not silently mutate or overwrite canonical history.
verification_evidence_required: Absence of destructive update paths for canonical records or explicit tombstone/event pattern
minimum_tests_required: Updates append new state or event records
Historical records remain queryable/reconstructable
Mutation attempts are rejected or represented as new lineage events

requirements.title: Causal memory lineage
requirements.summary: Memory entries must retain causal predecessor/correlation/lineage links sufficient to reconstruct how state evolved.
verification_evidence_required: Data model fields for lineage/correlation/predecessor references
minimum_tests_required: Lineage references are preserved on write
Replay reconstructs lineage-sensitive state
Missing lineage where mandatory fails or is explicitly marked non-causal

requirements.title: Deterministic replay
requirements.summary: Cortex must reconstruct state deterministically from the append-only substrate/outbox behavior.
minimum_tests_required: Same input history yields same replay state
Double-run proof output is byte-identical
Replay handles restart/retry scenarios

requirements.title: Transactional outbox state machine
requirements.summary: Outbox processing must preserve writes and deterministic indexing across pending, leased, processed, retry, restart, and lease-expiry paths.
minimum_tests_required: pending -> leased -> processed transition
lease expiry allows safe reprocessing
restart/retry does not lose writes
duplicate processing is idempotent
source_product_features: More Durable Outbox and Replay Behavior

requirements.title: Fail-closed tenant context validation
requirements.summary: Tenant context must be required and fail closed for missing, empty, whitespace, non-string, aliased, or fallback tenant values.
minimum_tests_required: missing tenant rejected
empty/whitespace tenant rejected
non-string tenant rejected
alias/fallback tenant rejected

requirements.title: Tenant isolation across query/provider boundaries
requirements.summary: Foreign provider/vector/query results must be filtered before crossing tenant boundaries.
verification_evidence_required: Negative tests for malicious foreign results
minimum_tests_required: Foreign tenant records cannot be returned
Provider returns mixed tenants and foreign rows are dropped
No silent widening of tenant scope

requirements.title: Authoritative document store precedes derivative index
requirements.summary: Authoritative document writes must precede derivative vector indexing; index outage must not corrupt authoritative memory.
verification_evidence_required: Failure handling when vector provider unavailable
minimum_tests_required: document write precedes indexing
vector outage keeps authoritative record valid
index failure does not mutate document store
source_product_features: Authoritative Document Store + Derivative Vector Index

requirements.title: Vector index is disposable derivative
requirements.summary: Vector index hits/misses/divergence must never become canonical truth or imply authoritative memory existence/nonexistence.
verification_evidence_required: Separation of authoritative store and vector payloads
minimum_tests_required: missing vector hit does not imply missing memory
payload divergence does not become canonical truth
derivative index can be reconstructed from authoritative records
source_product_features: Authoritative Document Store + Derivative Vector Index

requirements.title: Duplicate derivative upsert idempotency
requirements.summary: Duplicate derivative index upserts must be idempotent and must not create multiple canonical meanings.
minimum_tests_required: duplicate derivative upsert is idempotent
duplicate vector upsert cannot duplicate authoritative memory

requirements.title: Decay calculation deterministic and versioned
requirements.summary: Memory influence decay must be deterministic, explicit about calculation version, and affected predictably by configurable half-life.
minimum_tests_required: unused memory influence decays deterministically
half-life changes behavior predictably
same inputs produce same decay output

requirements.title: Reinforcement deterministic and bounded
requirements.summary: Retrieval reinforcement may increase influence but cannot make poisoned or low-trust memory authoritative by mass alone.
minimum_tests_required: successful retrieval increases influence deterministically
trust floors/ceilings prevent reinforcement-only promotion
decay/reinforcement composition deterministic

requirements.title: Retrieval influence receipts
requirements.summary: Retrieval influence receipts must preserve selected, excluded, ranking, candidate-set, and degradation evidence where applicable.
minimum_tests_required: selected candidates recorded
excluded candidates/reasons recorded where applicable
ranking evidence recorded
semantic degradation is explicit and not hidden

requirements.title: Temporal memory receipts stronger commitments
requirements.summary: Temporal receipts must carry commitment/versioning semantics sufficient to reconstruct and verify memory state over time.
minimum_tests_required: temporal receipt includes commitment version
receipt supports replay or temporal verification
receipt fields are deterministic

requirements.title: Fact identity semantics
requirements.summary: Fact identity must distinguish duplicate claims, reinforcement, and evolving fact slots without collapsing distinct lineage.
minimum_tests_required: duplicate fact does not create false new fact identity
reinforcement strengthens existing fact influence without losing lineage
evolving fact slot is distinguishable from duplicate

requirements.title: Governed mode Ed25519 signing
requirements.summary: Governed mode must sign receipts with Ed25519 and verify valid signed receipts.
minimum_tests_required: valid signed receipt verifies
unsigned open-core path remains allowed only where governed signing is not enforced

requirements.title: Governed signing tamper/key lifecycle rejection
requirements.summary: Verifier must reject tampered payloads and revoked keys while accepting retired historical keys according to registry semantics.
minimum_tests_required: tampered payload rejected
revoked key rejected
retired historical key accepted according to semantics
unknown key rejected

requirements.title: Evidence Pack deterministic manifest and hash tree
requirements.summary: Evidence Pack manifests/hash trees must be deterministic and tamper-detecting.
minimum_tests_required: manifest/hash tree deterministic
modified included artifact fails verification
double-run evidence is byte-identical

requirements.title: Invariant manifest I1-I12
requirements.summary: Invariant manifest must cover I1-I12 and report all checks pass only when each proof family is present and passing.
minimum_tests_required: manifest lists I1-I12
missing proof fails manifest
all_checks_passed only true when all included checks pass

requirements.title: Unified proof bundle composition
requirements.summary: Proof bundle must compose the existing I1-I12 proof artifacts without redefining proof semantics.
minimum_tests_required: bundle includes I1-I12 artifacts
bundle fails or reports incomplete if required artifact missing
bundle does not mutate constituent proof semantics
source_product_features: Cortex Proof Spine I1-I7 + Unified Proof Bundle

requirements.title: Content-addressed proof bundle identity
requirements.summary: proof_bundle_id must equal deterministic Merkle root/content hash identity.
minimum_tests_required: proof_bundle_id equals merkle_root
artifact hash order deterministic
tampered artifact changes root

requirements.title: Proof CLI command surface
requirements.summary: All claimed proof CLI commands must exist, run, support output where claimed, and emit deterministic JSON or documented output.
minimum_tests_required: each proof command is registered
--output writes file for commands that claim it
stdout JSON supported where claimed
exit code nonzero on failure where applicable

requirements.title: Byte-identical deterministic proof gates
requirements.summary: Proof commands that claim deterministic artifacts must be double-run diff gated in tests or CI.
verification_evidence_required: CI workflow or test command evidence
minimum_tests_required: individual proof artifacts double-run byte-identical
proof bundle double-run byte-identical
CI/test fails on nondeterminism

requirements.title: Portable auditor-grade proof artifact
requirements.summary: Auditor-facing proof claims require portable artifacts that can be generated and inspected without trusting screenshots or marketing copy.
minimum_tests_required: bundle can be generated locally
artifact contains enough metadata to inspect included proofs
docs avoid requiring trust in screenshots

requirements.title: Benchmark CLI deterministic reports
requirements.summary: Benchmark reports must be deterministic, CI-safe, provider-attributed, and generated through the Cortex benchmark command.
minimum_tests_required: cortex benchmark writes deterministic report
report includes provider_name/provider_kind/provider_version/fixture_version or equivalent
quality gate rules are stable and inspectable

requirements.title: Cortex must not be positioned as vector DB/RAG only
requirements.summary: Docs/copy must not reduce Cortex to vector search, RAG, or chat history, and must not present vector index as canonical truth.
minimum_tests_required: no 'vector index is canonical truth' claim
no RAG-only positioning
no contradiction of authoritative store over derivative index
requirements.false_claim_gate: Any public claim that the vector index is canonical truth is a critical contradiction.

requirements.title: Cortex must not claim Runtime execution governance ownership
requirements.summary: Cortex may preserve receipt-linked memory/evidence but must not claim to perform Runtime policy decisions, execution authorization, or MCP Gateway enforcement.
verification_evidence_required: No direct governed execution authority path
minimum_tests_required: no code path authorizes external execution as Cortex authority
docs state Cortex preserves/proves memory truth rather than decides/executes
Runtime/Gateway responsibilities not duplicated
requirements.false_claim_gate: Claiming Runtime execution governance inside Cortex is a scope violation and false claim.

requirements.title: Open-core unsigned receipts vs governed signing distinction
requirements.summary: Docs and code must preserve the distinction that unsigned open-core receipts may exist when governed signing is not enforced, while governed mode enforces signing.
minimum_tests_required: open-core path allows unsigned receipts only outside governed enforcement
governed mode rejects unsigned/tampered where required
docs do not imply signing is universally mandatory in open core

requirements.title: CI-uploaded proof/benchmark artifacts
requirements.summary: CI should upload deterministic proof and benchmark artifacts where the repo claims CI-backed proof/benchmark evidence.
minimum_tests_required: proof artifacts uploaded by CI
benchmark report artifacts uploaded where claimed
workflow runs on PR or protected main as appropriate

requirements.title: Python tooling hygiene for proof lanes
requirements.summary: Formatter/import/type-checker hygiene must remain bounded enough that proof/CI lanes do not drift silently.
verification_evidence_required: Version bounds for dev tools where claimed
minimum_tests_required: black/isort or equivalent clean on touched scope where configured
mypy/static typing command passes where claimed
dev-tool major version drift bounded

requirements.title: Documentation claim consistency with proof spine
requirements.summary: README/docs must not claim I1-I7, proof bundle, governed signing, or benchmark artifacts as complete unless code, CLI, tests, and artifacts support the claim.
minimum_tests_required: I1-I7 docs match invariant manifest
proof bundle docs match command output
deferred items are labeled deferred not complete
source_product_features: Cortex Proof Spine I1-I7 + Unified Proof Bundle
requirements.false_claim_gate: Unsupported proof-spine marketing claims are false_claim findings.

requirements.title: Evidentiary Memory category claim
requirements.summary: Cortex may claim Evidentiary Memory only when memory can testify through immutable spine records, hash verification, epoch-root proof, and replay evidence.
verification_evidence_required: Implementation of immutable spine records or equivalent canonical causal substrate
Proof artifacts tying memory to content hashes and epoch roots
Docs/copy using Evidentiary Memory and Verifiable Memory consistently
minimum_tests_required: memory record includes canonical spine/event reference
memory proof includes contentHash verification result
unsupported memory-only or vector-only testimony claim is flagged
requirements.false_claim_gate: Any claim that unverified summaries, embeddings, or narratives can testify without canonical anchors is a false claim.

requirements.title: Two-plane storage separation
requirements.summary: Plane 1 immutable spine records and Plane 2 mutable gravity weights must remain separate; gravity orders recall but never gates access to Plane 1.
verification_evidence_required: Recall path resolves ordered candidates through RecordStore
minimum_tests_required: RecordStore exposes no update/delete API
zeroing gravity weight does not remove RecordStore.get or scan result
recall always performs contentHash verification before returning memory

requirements.title: Spine record immutability and content hashing
requirements.summary: Spine events must be immutable and permanent, with contentHash computed from canonical serialization.
verification_evidence_required: Append path computes or validates contentHash
minimum_tests_required: attempted mutation is impossible by interface or rejected
contentHash changes when canonical event bytes change
canonical JSON fixtures are stable across field-order variation

requirements.title: IMemory recall lens verification
requirements.summary: IMemory must be a recall lens over a spine event and must add no new truth beyond verification metadata.
minimum_tests_required: IMemory includes event reference and verification metadata
verification failure is explicit and never silently included as valid
IMemory model does not contain independent truth payload that can outrank the event

requirements.title: Epoch Root hierarchy and late-arrival semantics
requirements.summary: Epoch roots must follow spine to day to week to quarter hierarchy, close on UTC boundaries, and never recompute closed roots for late-arriving events.
minimum_tests_required: same events ordered by occurredAt/id produce same spine root
late event for closed day records in current open day with attestedLate original date
closed day/week/quarter roots are not recomputed

requirements.title: Offline inclusion proof verification
requirements.summary: An event must be verifiable against an epoch root using only event bytes, proof, and root, without store access.
minimum_tests_required: single event verifies against quarter root with no store access
mutating historical event byte breaks all relevant levels
deleting day data breaks containing week root

requirements.title: Hash tree construction safety
requirements.summary: Epoch hash trees must use domain-separated leaf/node hashes, promote odd nodes without duplicate-last, and define empty-day sentinel roots.
verification_evidence_required: Leaf and node prefix/domain separation evidence
minimum_tests_required: leaf/node domain separation fixture passes
odd-node duplicate-last is rejected by test
empty day sentinel root stable and documented

requirements.title: Gravitational Recall v1 fixed force taxonomy
requirements.summary: Gravity v1 must score recall with the fixed force taxonomy: occurrence, recall, recency, and association.
minimum_tests_required: all four force fields are present
unapproved force taxonomy extension fails contract test
score total is bounded within [0,1] or explicitly normalized

requirements.title: Gravity deterministic explicit-time scoring
requirements.summary: Gravity scoring must be deterministic with explicit asOf input and no hidden randomness or wall-clock time.
verification_evidence_required: score(memoryId, asOf, params, history) signature or equivalent
minimum_tests_required: 1000 generated histories score twice with byte-identical output
static/lint test blocks hidden now/random in scoring path
same inputs after process restart produce same output

requirements.title: Gravity hash-pinned params and emitted function identity
requirements.summary: Every GravityScore must record gravityVersion and gravityFunctionHash derived from version plus canonical params.
minimum_tests_required: changing any param changes gravityFunctionHash
same params produce same hash across runs
score output records the exact hash used

requirements.title: Semantic bootstrap blend remains subordinate to gravity proof
requirements.summary: Semantic bootstrap may blend with gravity for sparse tenants, but recall output must carry full GravityScore and deterministic blend metadata.
verification_evidence_required: Pinned crossover function of tenant history depth
RecallService ordering by blended score with GravityScore breakdown
minimum_tests_required: semantic scorer is pluggable and not canonical truth
history-depth crossover is deterministic
recall result records gravity score breakdown and blend metadata

requirements.title: Production recall events are spine records
requirements.summary: Every production recall must write a Recall spine event capturing query, asOf, gravity version/hash, params hash, result set, scores, and epochRef.
verification_evidence_required: Event payload includes scoring and epoch metadata
minimum_tests_required: recall writes kind=Recall spine event
event includes query/asOf/gravityVersion/gravityFunctionHash/paramsHash
event includes result set memoryIds with scores and epochRef

requirements.title: ReplayService reconstructs pinned historical recall
requirements.summary: ReplayService must rebuild HistoryView as of the original recall timestamp and recompute with the pinned gravity version and params.
minimum_tests_required: production recall to replay matches true across generated scenarios
gravity/1.0.0 recall still replays under 1.0.0 after 1.1.0 exists
mismatch produces explicit diff finding

requirements.title: Counterfactual recall purity
requirements.summary: Counterfactual recall must run in read-only sandboxed HistoryView modes and must not mutate Plane 1 or Plane 2.
verification_evidence_required: Store spy tests proving no writes
minimum_tests_required: withoutMemory reports changed or unchanged result set explicitly
forceMemory includes gravity gap explanation
withParams simulates alternate params without production state mutation
counterfactual run writes nothing to RecordStore or GravityIndex

requirements.title: Recall replay and counterfactual Evidence Pack entries
requirements.summary: Evidence Packs must be able to include recall replay and counterfactual results as structured, epoch-referenced artifacts.
verification_evidence_required: Artifact schema with recallEventId and epochRef
Verification path for included recall replay artifact
minimum_tests_required: recall replay artifact exports with epochRef
counterfactual artifact exports with mode and result diff
tampering exported artifact changes pack verification/root

requirements.title: Deliberation records are cognitive spine artifacts
requirements.summary: Collective deliberation snapshots may be stored as Deliberation spine events but must be explicitly cognitive and distinct from execution receipts.
verification_evidence_required: delib_ identifier prefix and namespace separation from rcpt_
minimum_tests_required: stored DeliberationRecord includes branches/reviewer verdicts/heat/dissent/witness narrative ref
identifier prefix is delib_ not rcpt_
rendering or schema distinguishes cognitive record from receipt

requirements.title: Receipt verification does not consult deliberation
requirements.summary: Receipt verification must never import, call, or require deliberation modules or records.
minimum_tests_required: receipt verification path has zero imports/calls into deliberation modules
receipt with deliberationRef verifies solely from receipt evidence
missing deliberation record does not make valid receipt invalid
requirements.false_claim_gate: Any verification path requiring deliberation material to prove receipt validity is a critical doctrine violation.

requirements.title: Heat as versioned trust anchor input
requirements.summary: Cortex may expose Collective heat as a policy-evaluable trust anchor input, but heat remains versioned, hash-pinned, and non-authorizing by itself.
minimum_tests_required: heat provider returns score/consensus/version/hash
changing heat weights changes heatFunctionHash
low-consensus effect-bound action requires co-sign or fails closed in policy integration tests where Runtime contract is stubbed

requirements.title: Narrative corroboration remains anchored
requirements.summary: Witness or narrative factual claims stored through Cortex should carry optional spineRefs and a record-level corroboration status.
verification_evidence_required: spineRefs per factual claim or equivalent
minimum_tests_required: full coverage computes full corroboration
partial coverage computes partial
uncorroborated narratives cannot be treated as receipt truth

requirements.title: Doctrine vocabulary lint
requirements.summary: Cortex code, comments, and docs for this substrate must use approved vocabulary and avoid blockchain, ledger, or chain terminology.
verification_evidence_required: Repo-wide naming lint or forbidden-term test
minimum_tests_required: no public symbol contains blockchain/ledger/chain for this substrate
approved terms tamper-evident hash tree/transparency log/attestation are used where applicable
lint fails on forbidden vocabulary in touched scope
requirements.false_claim_gate: Using blockchain, ledger, or chain for this substrate is a naming and positioning violation unless explicitly isolated to third-party integration references.

required_negative_tests.scenario: missing tenant context is rejected fail-closed
empty/whitespace/non-string tenant context is rejected
foreign tenant provider/vector result is filtered and never returned
duplicate ingestion or replay does not create duplicate authoritative shards
leased outbox item after restart/lease expiry is safely reprocessed without lost writes
vector index outage does not corrupt or roll back authoritative memory
missing vector hit does not imply missing authoritative memory
divergent vector payload cannot become canonical truth
poisoned or low-trust memory cannot become authoritative by reinforcement volume alone
tampered governed receipt payload fails verification
revoked governed signing key fails verification
modified Evidence Pack artifact fails verification
missing/tampered proof bundle artifact fails or changes Merkle root
nondeterministic proof artifact double-run diff fails the gate
any Cortex code/doc path claiming direct execution authorization is flagged as false claim
zeroed gravity weight does not remove or hide the immutable spine record
contentHash verification failure is explicit and is never silently returned as valid memory
late-arriving event for closed day does not recompute the closed day root
mutated event bytes fail offline inclusion proof verification against quarter root
odd hash-tree node duplicate-last behavior is rejected
hidden wall-clock or random input in gravity scoring path fails lint/test
changing gravity params changes gravityFunctionHash
historical recall under old gravity version does not replay with newer gravity version
counterfactual recall does not write to Plane 1 or Plane 2
receipt verification path importing or consulting deliberation modules is rejected
heat score alone cannot authorize an effect-bound action
forbidden blockchain/ledger/chain vocabulary in Cortex substrate identifiers/docs fails lint

false_claim_gates.claim: Cortex proof spine I1-I7 is complete
false_claim_gates.fail_if: Any invariant family is absent, not runnable, not deterministic, or only documented.
Replay proof is missing, nondeterministic, or does not cover retry/restart-style scenarios.
Missing tenant falls back, foreign provider results leak, or tenant checks are docs-only.
Vector hit/miss can override canonical document truth.

false_claim_gates.claim: Governed signing/evidence pack integrity is implemented
false_claim_gates.fail_if: Governed signing is a stub, accepts tampered artifacts, or lacks key lifecycle tests.
Bundle is screenshots/docs only, not content-addressed, or cannot be generated locally.

required_evidence: None; this claim is out of scope and should be rejected
false_claim_gates.fail_if: Cortex claims Runtime/MCP Gateway execution authorization ownership.
The claim is supported only by vector search, summaries, chat history, or docs.
Roots are mutable, closed roots are recomputed, proofs require database trust, or tree construction is underspecified.

false_claim_gates.claim: Gravitational Recall is deterministic and inspectable
false_claim_gates.fail_if: Scoring uses hidden now/randomness, unversioned params, or unrecorded force behavior.
Replay uses current params/version, omits result scores, mutates production state, or swallows mismatch findings.

false_claim_gates.claim: Collective deliberation is preserved without becoming authority
false_claim_gates.fail_if: Deliberation is required for receipt verification, presented as execution proof, or heat authorizes effects by itself.

artifact_verification_rules: When a proof artifact claims byte-identical determinism, run the command twice and compare bytes.
When a proof bundle claims content-addressed identity, verify proof_bundle_id equals merkle_root.
When a Merkle/hash tree is claimed, verify deterministic sort/canonicalization before hashing.
When an Evidence Pack claims tamper detection, mutate an included artifact and require verification failure.
When CI-backed claims are made, inspect workflow commands and artifact upload steps, not just local scripts.
When Evidentiary Memory is claimed, verify the memory can testify through spine record, content hash, epoch root or proof artifact, not through summary text alone.
When Epoch Roots are claimed, verify inclusion proof without store access using only event bytes, proof, and root.
When Gravity scoring is claimed deterministic, verify no hidden wall-clock or randomness is reachable from scoring paths.
When replayable recall is claimed, verify replay uses the original gravity version and params hash, not the latest defaults.
When counterfactual recall is claimed, assert no writes to Plane 1 or Plane 2.
When deliberation linkage is claimed, verify receipt verification does not import or call deliberation modules.

remediation_lane_groups.lane: Lane A - Identity, Idempotency, and Append-Only Memory
remediation_lane_groups.boundary: No signing, CI, or benchmark work.

remediation_lane_groups.lane: Lane B - Replay, Outbox, and Durability
remediation_lane_groups.boundary: No tenant isolation or governed signing changes.

remediation_lane_groups.lane: Lane C - Tenant Isolation Negative Proofs
remediation_lane_groups.boundary: No broad query rewrite outside tenant enforcement seams.

remediation_lane_groups.lane: Lane D - Authority vs Derivative Index
remediation_lane_groups.boundary: Do not change canonical store semantics outside needed authority/index seams.

remediation_lane_groups.lane: Lane E - Decay, Reinforcement, and Influence Receipts
Lane F - Governed Signing and Evidence Pack Integrity
Lane G - Proof Bundle, CLI, Manifest, and Determinism Gates
remediation_lane_groups.boundary: Compose existing proofs; do not redefine underlying invariant semantics.

remediation_lane_groups.lane: Lane H - Benchmark, CI, and Tooling Hygiene
remediation_lane_groups.boundary: No behavioral proof semantics unless needed to make benchmark/tooling claims truthful.

remediation_lane_groups.lane: Lane I - Scope and False-Claim Cleanup
remediation_lane_groups.boundary: Docs/copy/API claims only unless code contradicts scope.

remediation_lane_groups.lane: Lane J - Evidentiary Memory and Two-Plane Storage
remediation_lane_groups.boundary: No gravity computation, epoch tree construction, replay, or Collective bridge behavior beyond shared interfaces.

remediation_lane_groups.lane: Lane K - Epoch Roots and Offline Inclusion Proofs
remediation_lane_groups.boundary: No external anchoring, transparency-log service, governance authorization, or recall scoring changes.
No counterfactual replay, quarantine workflow, tenant tuning UI, or policy authorization logic.

remediation_lane_groups.lane: Lane M - Replayable and Counterfactual Recall
remediation_lane_groups.boundary: Read-only cognition over spine records. No production state writes in counterfactual paths.

remediation_lane_groups.lane: Lane N - Collective Bridge and Doctrine Vocabulary
remediation_lane_groups.boundary: Preserve deliberation as cognitive material only. Do not make deliberation, heat, or narratives into execution receipts or authorization proof.

verdict_policy.conformant: All critical and high requirements pass with direct implementation/test/proof evidence, including I1-I12; medium/low gaps are documented and non-contradictory.
verdict_policy.conditionally_conformant: No critical gaps or false claims; high gaps are limited, non-security, and have explicit remediation lanes.
verdict_policy.non_conformant: Any critical requirement missing/contradicted, any proof-spine or Evidentiary Memory false claim, any tenant leakage, any vector-as-authority contradiction, any broken governed signing integrity claim, any mutable closed epoch root, any nondeterministic gravity scoring path, any counterfactual production write, any receipt verification dependency on deliberation, or any Cortex direct-execution-governance claim.
